verdict.finding: R4 kill-metric semantics (first-poll measurement book vs operated arm)
verdict.deterministic_core: exact reproduction: 36 records -> 15 sized / 12 rich-first-poll-entered-later / 9 never-entered; 27 ledger impulse_v2 trades map 1:1 by t0, zero anomalies, zero side mismatches, one trade per interval (dup guard in risk_ok makes a second trigger impossible), all 12 re-entries strictly later (entrySec > first-poll sec) and strictly cheaper (mean 11.19c/share cost improvement).

merge_agent_flaw_resolution.same_signal: YES — contrarian side is fixed per interval by the prior interval's move; all 12 sides match; ledger has exactly one impulse_v2 trade per t0; these are refills of the same signal at a later in-window poll, not second triggers.
merge_agent_flaw_resolution.deliberate_design: LARGELY YES — FINAL-DESIGN.md MF6 pre-registers the measurement book as first-fillable-poll recording ('matches the CONFIRMED fast-entry regime') and Phase-1 kill + guard tiers + qhat are all pre-registered on that book; the ~43% sized fraction (composition divergence) was explicitly anticipated in section 4.3. What was NOT anticipated: post-skip re-poll price improvement (the 5.2c deterministic component), which biases the kill input below the operated arm beyond the anticipated composition effect. Bot faithfully implements the design; therefore this is an AMENDMENT REQUEST to a pre-registered protocol, not an implementation bug. R4's wording 'misclassifies ... as skips' overstates: sized flag means 'sized at first fillable poll' per its registered definition.

corrections: Gap decomposition: only 5.16c/share of the 9.8c is deterministic (price improvement on the 12 re-entries); 4.64c is composition (dropping the 9 never-entered) and outcome-dependent. Under outcome permutation the expected structural gap is ~6.5c (90% of permutations positive); observed 9.8c is unremarkable vs that null (p=0.39). Honest structural effect: ~5-6.5c, still >2x the 2c kill bar, so 'kill can fire on a positive policy' stands.
Their CI90 for the -6.23c book ([-15.35,+3.33]) is understated; iid bootstrap gives [-19.73,+7.60]. Does not change any conclusion (straddles 0 either way; n=35 << 200 so kill not evaluable).

STRENGTHENING: the nightly guard tiers (haircut 7d<-2c n>=120; bench 7d<-4c n>=120 / 15d<-3c n>=250, bot _impulse_nightly netps()) read the SAME first-poll book and hit their n-minimums BEFORE day-14; the structural bias reaches the guards first. qhat also buckets by first-poll cost, so re-entered fills train the wrong price bucket.
verdict.selection_audit: No fitted parameters, no grid, no TRAIN/TEST split applicable — this is a live instrumentation audit. Class boundaries (entered vs not) are natural, not searched. The finding was 1 of ~13 aspects the flagship agent audited (K~13), but the core claims are deterministic counts immune to Bonferroni; the noisy EV point estimates carry wide CIs already reported. No look-ahead: all quantities computed from decision-time records and settled outcomes.
